metadata.title:
LumDePmen Coming


10 Spokane

Program Of Address at the Meeting
of Sawmill 1Een-Retailers Also
Coming This Wssk--Hoo Hoo Con-
catenatton.

pages.raw_text:
Lumbermen from all parts of the
northwest will be in the city this week,
Lambelman great and lumbermen
small, lumbermen who wholesale and
those who retail, sawmillmen and con-
tractors, consumers and shippers will
be here for three days. The Western
Pine Shippers association holds its
annual meeting and election of officers
tomorrow; the Inland Empire Lumber
mems association will be in session
Wednesday and Thursday night and
Wednesday night there will be given
banquet by the Spokane iumbermen
to the visitors. Thursday night there
will be confederation of the Hoo
Hoos. This is a secret society of the
lumbermen and men who are in touch
with the numbering interests.


The Western Pine Shippers associa-
ton meets tomorrow afternoon at
o'clock in the Offices of the secretary,
r. A. kellogg, in the Golden Gate
block. Reports viii be received from
President G. w. Mason, of the Occur
d'Alene Lumber company, from R. A.
Kellogg, the secretary, and from w. =.
Ufford, the treasurer. The chairman
of the railroad committee will also
report.


The most cheerful recent statement
of the business outlook for the lumber
ing interests for this section is made
by the secretary of the association, in
his call for the meeting. He says:


The outlook for the coming year
seems to be better that at any time for
several months. Many of our members
report orders coming in more freely
crop reports on winter wheat show
tiattering conditions, building and
heavy construction projects seem to
be above the average, and money is
easier"


The program of addresses for the
meeting tomorrow afternoon is as
follows:


Marketing Our Products" A. M.
Fox; Uniform Grading" Petrol Davis;
Logging in the Inland Empire, T"
Austin Redding; Labor Problems"
William Deary; How to Obtain a
Reduction in Freight Rates. w. C.
Ufford; Associations" f. F. Cartier
Van Dissel; what are Our Products, !"
A, f. Rickerd.


The meeting of the retail lumbermen
will last two days, commencing We'd
nesday. Besides the members of the
association, retail dealers from all
parts of the four Pacific northwestern
states have been invited and are ex-
pected in large numbers. The railr-
roads have granted reduced rates.
